title: Ukraine's Donbas rear area, from Sloviansk to Kramatorsk, is under constant Russian pressure
subhead: 'Donbas: The War of Attrition' (1/2). Just a few kilometers from one of Ukraine's fiercest fronts, these two cities are under drone strikes that make residents' lives very difficult. Both civilians and soldiers fear the bombings will intensify.

title: 'I almost faint every time I stand up': In cramped student housing, studying becomes impossible during the heatwave

How cities in Africa are adapting to intense and deadly heat
subhead: Reflective paints, green roofs, work breaks: Many African countries are seeking innovative ways to cope with increasingly extreme temperatures.

title: Iran intensifies internal crackdown as US and Israel wage war on regime
subhead: While diplomatic talks between Tehran and Washington focus on Iran's nuclear program and sanctions, NGOs are warning that executions and mass arrests continue.

title: Sam Barlow: The art of fragmented storytelling from the creator of 'Her Story' and 'Immortality'
subhead: Since 'Her Story' (2015), the video game designer has subverted traditional narrative mechanics, immersing players in a profusion of video fragments and leaving them to piece the story back together.

title: French tolerance remains strong, but racism and antisemitism still run deep, report finds
subhead: In its annual report released on Thursday, the National Consultative Commission on Human Rights expresses concern about the persistence of xenophobic prejudices in French society, particularly among the younger generation.

subhead: A shooting in the Canadian city on Monday, June 22, left three dead, including a police officer and the attacker. In a manifesto, the shooter expressed his hatred of women.

title: Serving a husband, God and America: Young female conservatives gather at San Antonio's Turning Point summit
subhead: In early June, around 3,000 women attended the Women's Leadership Summit in San Antonio, Texas. This annual event brings together a variety of conservative figures and influencers, relying on the extensive network of Turning Point USA, founded by right-wing activist Charlie Kirk, who was murdered in 2025. After convincing a majority of young men to vote for Trump in 2024, the organization now targets female voters from Gen Z.

title: France faces unprecedented heatwave: 'We are about to experience four of the hottest days ever recorded in the country'
subhead: A prolonged heatwave, rather than a brief spike, has arrived. Unusually high temperatures are set to persist across the country until at least the end of this week, exposing more than 90% of the French population to extreme and rare heat.
